observations.description: Qwen selected for FP-010 based on schema integration quality. Qwen modeled packet_id as INTEGER FK to packets(id), which was more relationally consistent with ForgePilot's existing SQLite design.

DeepSeek selected for FP-011 based on packet vocabulary and schema alignment. DeepSeek used validation_state/admission_state vocabulary directly matching the packet and updated the canonical schema.sql.
DeepSeek selected for FP-012 based on evidence-first and schema-bound requirements. DeepSeek used explicit relational columns, canonical schema integration, and narrower correction delta.
DeepSeek required 3 corrections on FP-010: validation_state allowed NOT_EVALUATED, trust_tier allowed empty string, TypeScript validation used truthy check instead of !== undefined.
Qwen self-corrected 1 truthy-check validation bug on FP-010, adding 2 tests. Fewer corrections than DeepSeek on this packet.
DeepSeek had 1 fix attempt on FP-011: initial admission evaluation returned admission outcomes in a validation_state field. Corrected by separating result types.
Qwen had 2 fix attempts on FP-011 and incurred observed cost of $2.61 USD. Used validation_outcome/admission_outcome naming instead of packet's direct vocabulary.

Qwen required a larger correction pass on FP-012: initial JSON-array-centered design had weaker relational guarantees. Correction added explicit columns, model_identity, correction tables, and a second migration.
DeepSeek had 1 narrow post-audit correction on FP-012: recordModelComparisonCorrection did not carry forward model_a_defects and model_b_defects. Fix was 2 lines changed, 1 regression test added.
Both models maintained scope discipline across all three packets. No audit found routing, ranking, leaderboards, dashboards, reports, cost optimization, or other forbidden behavior.
observations.evidence_basis: All audit artifacts in runs/FP-010/, runs/FP-011/, runs/FP-012/

observations.description: Both models achieved accepted audit status on all three packets. No implementation was rejected.

Higher test counts did not correlate with selection. FP-010: DeepSeek had more tests (28 vs 24) but Qwen selected. FP-011: Qwen had more tests (57 vs 40) but DeepSeek selected. FP-012: Qwen had more tests (375 vs 344) but DeepSeek selected.
In all three packets, the selected implementation was the one that more closely integrated with ForgePilot's existing SQLite relational model and matched packet vocabulary.

DeepSeek required more corrections on the simpler persistence task (FP-010: 3 issues) but fewer on complex protocol tasks (FP-011: 1, FP-012: 1 narrow fix). Qwen required fewer corrections on FP-010 (1) but more on complex tasks (FP-011: 2, FP-012: large correction pass).

Qwen's getLatestTaskClassification() and getLatestModelComparison() return the latest event row but do not merge correction data. After corrections are recorded, these functions still return the original uncorrected event. Identified as a design gap, not an AC violation.
Qwen FP-011 execution incurred observed cost of $2.61 USD. This is the only explicit cost observation recorded across all three packets.

limitations: Only 3 packets reviewed (FP-010, FP-011, FP-012). Patterns may not generalize.
Only 2 models compared (Qwen 3.7 Max, DeepSeek V4 Pro High).
All 3 packets are schema-bound persistence tasks. No coverage of CLI, refactoring, bugfix, or documentation-only tasks.
Single execution per model per packet. No repeated executions to measure consistency or variance.
Cost data is sparse. Only 1 explicit cost observation ($2.61 for Qwen on FP-011).
Cross-model audit: each model audited the other. Audit quality may reflect auditor model interpretation.
Correction circumstances differ (self-discovered vs audit-identified, pre-commit vs post-audit). Direct correction burden comparison is limited.
Eval-comparison packets (FP-META-010, FP-META-011, FP-META-012) are separate evidence and not conflated with this review.
non_decisions: This review does not establish a global model ranking.
This review does not establish a routing policy.
This review does not establish a provider recommendation.
This review does not establish a cost optimization rule.
This review does not establish an automatic model-selection signal.
This review does not establish a leaderboard result.
This review does not establish a preference for one model across all task types.
Observed patterns are limited to the reviewed packets and must not be extrapolated beyond the evidence.
